references.text:
[Er50c] Erd\H{o}s, P., Az $1/x_1 + 1/x_2 + \ldots + 1/x_n =A/B$ egyenlet eg\'{e}sz sz\'{a}m\'{u} megold\'{a}sair\'{o}l. Mat. Lapok (1950), 192-210.
 
 [Vo85] Vose, Michael D., Egyptian fractions. Bull. London Math. Soc. (1985), 21-24.
 
 [vDTa25b] W. van Doorn and Q. Tang, The smallest denominator not contained in a unit fraction decomposition of 1 with fixed length. arXiv:2512.22083 (2025).

remarks.text:
Erd\H{o}s \cite{Er50c} proved that\[\log\log b \ll N(b) \ll \frac{\log b}{\log\log b}.\]The upper bound was improved by Vose \cite{Vo85} to\[N(b) \ll \sqrt{\log b}.\]One can also investigate the average of $N(a,b)$ for fixed $b$, and it is known that\[\frac{1}{b}\sum_{1\leq a<b}N(a,b) \gg \log\log b.\]Related to [18]. There is also a close connection to [293] (particularly with $N(b-1,b)$), as elucidated by van Doorn and Tang \cite{vDTa25b}.

This problem has been formalised in Lean as part of the Google DeepMind Formal Conjectures project.

statement.text: For integers $1\leq a<b$ let $N(a,b)$ denote the minimal $k$ such that there exist integers $1<n_1<\cdots<n_k$ with\[\frac{a}{b}=\frac{1}{n_1}+\cdots+\frac{1}{n_k}.\]Estimate $N(b)=\max_{1\leq a<b}N(a,b)$. Is it true that $N(b) \ll \log\log b$?
